options.description: The ISO-3166-1 country code of the country from which to read phone numbers

suggestions.name: BQ - Bonaire, Sint Eustatius and Saba
CD - Congo, Democratic Republic of the
GB - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
GS - South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
HM - Heard Island and McDonald Islands

options.name: --account-sid
options.description: The SID of the Account requesting the AvailablePhoneNumber resources

options.name: --address-sid
options.description: The SID of the Address resource we should associate with the new phone number. Some regions require addresses to meet local regulations

options.name: --api-version
options.description: The API version to use for incoming calls made to the new phone number. The default is `2010-04-01`

options.name: --area-code
options.description: The area code of the phone numbers to read. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --beta
options.description: Whether to read phone numbers that are new to the Twilio platform. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `true`

options.name: --no-beta
options.description: Whether to read phone numbers that are new to the Twilio platform. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `true`

options.name: --bundle-sid
options.description: The SID of the Bundle resource that you associate with the phone number. Some regions require a Bundle to meet local Regulations

options.name: --contains
options.description: The pattern on which to match phone numbers. Valid characters are `*`, `0-9`, `a-z`, and `A-Z`. The `*` character matches any single digit. For examples, see Example 2 and Example 3. If specified, this value must have at least two characters

options.name: --distance
options.description: The search radius, in miles, for a `near_` query.  Can be up to `500` and the default is `25`. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --emergency-address-sid
options.description: The SID of the emergency address configuration to use for emergency calling from the new phone number

options.name: --emergency-status
options.description: The parameter displays if emergency calling is enabled for this number. Active numbers may place emergency calls by dialing valid emergency numbers for the country

options.name: --exclude-all-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require an Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --no-exclude-all-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require an Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --exclude-foreign-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require a foreign Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --no-exclude-foreign-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require a foreign Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --exclude-local-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require a local Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --no-exclude-local-address-required
options.description: Whether to exclude phone numbers that require a local Address. Can be: `true` or `false` and the default is `false`

options.name: --fax-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive faxes. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --no-fax-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive faxes. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --friendly-name
options.description: A descriptive string that you created to describe the new phone number. It can be up to 64 characters long. By default, this is a formatted version of the new phone number

options.name: --identity-sid
options.description: The SID of the Identity resource that we should associate with the new phone number. Some regions require an identity to meet local regulations

options.name: --in-lata
options.description: Limit results to a specific local access and transport area (LATA). Given a phone number, search within the same LATA as that number. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --in-locality
options.description: Limit results to a particular locality or city. Given a phone number, search within the same Locality as that number

options.name: --in-postal-code
options.description: Limit results to a particular postal code. Given a phone number, search within the same postal code as that number. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --in-rate-center
options.description: Limit results to a specific rate center, or given a phone number search within the same rate center as that number. Requires `in_lata` to be set as well. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --in-region
options.description: Limit results to a particular region, state, or province. Given a phone number, search within the same region as that number. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --limit
options.description: The maximum number of resources to return. Use '--no-limit' to disable

options.name: --mms-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive MMS messages. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --no-mms-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive MMS messages. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --near-lat-long
options.description: Given a latitude/longitude pair `lat,long` find geographically close numbers within `distance` miles. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --near-number
options.description: Given a phone number, find a geographically close number within `distance` miles. Distance defaults to 25 miles. Applies to only phone numbers in the US and Canada

options.name: --no-header
options.description: Skip including of headers while listing the data

options.name: --page-size
options.description: How many resources to return in each list page. The default is 50, and the maximum is 1000

options.name: --properties
options.description: The properties you would like to display (JSON output always shows all properties)

options.name: --sms-application-sid
options.description: The SID of the application that should handle SMS messages sent to the new phone number. If an `sms_application_sid` is present, we ignore all of the `sms_*_url` urls and use those set on the application

options.name: --sms-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive text messages. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --no-sms-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive text messages. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --sms-fallback-method
options.description: The HTTP method that we should use to call `sms_fallback_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`

options.name: --sms-fallback-url
options.description: The URL that we should call when an error occurs while requesting or executing the TwiML defined by `sms_url`

options.name: --sms-method
options.description: The HTTP method that we should use to call `sms_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`

options.name: --sms-url
options.description: The URL we should call when the new phone number receives an incoming SMS message

options.name: --status-callback
options.description: The URL we should call using the `status_callback_method` to send status information to your application

options.name: --status-callback-method
options.description: The HTTP method we should use to call `status_callback`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`

options.name: --trunk-sid
options.description: The SID of the Trunk we should use to handle calls to the new phone number. If a `trunk_sid` is present, we ignore all of the voice urls and voice applications and use only those set on the Trunk. Setting a `trunk_sid` will automatically delete your `voice_application_sid` and vice versa

options.name: --voice-application-sid
options.description: The SID of the application we should use to handle calls to the new phone number. If a `voice_application_sid` is present, we ignore all of the voice urls and use only those set on the application. Setting a `voice_application_sid` will automatically delete your `trunk_sid` and vice versa

options.name: --voice-caller-id-lookup
options.description: Whether to lookup the caller's name from the CNAM database and post it to your app. Can be: `true` or `false` and defaults to `false`

options.name: --no-voice-caller-id-lookup
options.description: Whether to lookup the caller's name from the CNAM database and post it to your app. Can be: `true` or `false` and defaults to `false`

options.name: --voice-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive calls. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --no-voice-enabled
options.description: Whether the phone numbers can receive calls. Can be: `true` or `false`

options.name: --voice-fallback-method
options.description: The HTTP method that we should use to call `voice_fallback_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`

options.name: --voice-fallback-url
options.description: The URL that we should call when an error occurs retrieving or executing the TwiML requested by `url`

options.name: --voice-method
options.description: The HTTP method that we should use to call `voice_url`. Can be: `GET` or `POST` and defaults to `POST`

options.name: --voice-receive-mode
options.description: The configuration parameter for the new phone number to receive incoming voice calls or faxes. Can be: `fax` or `voice` and defaults to `voice`

options.name: --voice-url
options.description: The URL that we should call to answer a call to the new phone number. The `voice_url` will not be called if a `voice_application_sid` or a `trunk_sid` is set

options.description: Format of command output

options.description: Suppress output and logs. This is a shorthand for "-l none -o none"

options.name: --country-code
options.description: The ISO-3166-1 country code of the country from which to read phone numbers
